message:
test(fcp-conformance/hello-retry-cookie): pin compute_cookie/verify_cookie transcript binding (br-xukzs)

The stateless HelloRetry cookie is the responder's anti-DoS MAC: it
binds the entire Hello transcript (excluding hello.cookie and
hello.signature) under the responder's private cookie key so a
captured cookie cannot be replayed against a different Hello.

These properties live inline in fcp-protocol/src/session.rs but had no
cross-crate conformance coverage. A regression that drops one of the
bound fields from the MAC input would silently allow cookie replay
against tampered hellos.

Adds 10 tests:

- compute_then_verify_round_trips (happy path)
- cookie_issued_under_a_different_key_is_rejected
- flipped_cookie_byte_is_rejected
- cookie_binds_to_hello_from
- cookie_binds_to_hello_to
- cookie_binds_to_hello_eph_pubkey (entropy binding — prevents replay
  against a fresh ephemeral)
- cookie_binds_to_hello_nonce
- cookie_binds_to_hello_timestamp
- cookie_binds_to_hello_suites (suite-downgrade replay defense)
- compute_cookie_is_deterministic_for_fixed_inputs (the verifier
  recomputes-and-compares; non-determinism would break the contract)

All 10 use only the public fcp_protocol::session API. Verified locally
via cargo test -p fcp-conformance --test
session_hello_retry_cookie_conformance (10/10 in <0.01s).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
